title: Pentacalia jahnii
intro: Pentacalia jahnii, also known as Jahn's pentacalia, is a plant species in the Asteraceae family, native to the Andean region of South America. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Pentacalia jahnii include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Pentacalia jahnii is commonly used to make herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and essential oils, which are used to treat various health conditions and promote well-being.
taxonomy: Pentacalia jahnii belongs to the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. This classification reflects its relationship to other flowering plants within the asterid clade. As a member of the genus Pentacalia, Pentacalia jahnii is also part of the tribe Senecioneae. Within this tribe, Pentacalia jahnii is further distinguished from other species by its unique characteristics. Pentacalia jahnii is native to Ecuador, which adds to its specific classification within the plant kingdom.
